prerequisites: A KYVE storage pool ID (Pool-ID) identifying the pool whose data you want to extract. Browse available pools on the KYVE network to find the relevant Pool-ID.
A starting bundle ID (Bundle-Start-ID) marking the first finalized bundle to read from each pool.

A KYVE-API URL Base endpoint. By default the connector targets the official KYVE mainnet endpoint; you may override it to point at a different network (for example a testnet or devnet) or a self-hosted node.
No account, API key, or credentials are required: the KYVE REST/Trustless API is free and public with no query limit.

authMethods.name: No authentication (public API)
authMethods.summary: The connector has no secret fields. KYVE's REST/Trustless API is open and free to query without an API key, token, or credentials. Access is configured entirely through the public url_base endpoint plus the pool and bundle identifiers.

setupSteps.title: Identify the pool(s) to extract
setupSteps.body: Determine the Pool-ID(s) of the KYVE storage pool(s) you want to retrieve data from and enter them in the pool_ids field. To fetch multiple pools at once, provide comma-separated Pool-IDs.

setupSteps.title: Set the bundle start point
setupSteps.body: In start_ids, provide the Bundle-Start-ID for each pool to mark where extraction begins. When configuring multiple pools, supply matching comma-separated Bundle-Start-IDs aligned with the Pool-IDs in pool_ids.

setupSteps.title: Configure the API endpoint
setupSteps.body: Set url_base to the KYVE-API URL Base. Leave it at the official KYVE mainnet endpoint to read production data, or override it to target a different KYVE network or a node you operate.

setupSteps.title: Tune pagination (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set page_size to control how many records are requested per API page, and max_pages to cap the total number of pages retrieved per sync. Both fields are optional and can be left unset to use defaults.
